_comment: Small hand-curated supplements to the auto-derived index dashboard. These are the few items not derivable from the audit set itself: the 'Coming next' pending card (Phase 1.7 hasn't run yet, so it has no audit), and the cross-audit architectural callout. Keep this file thin; everything else flows from the audits.
pending_card_html:
Coming next

Does an AI agent then write correct Swift?

Test 1.7 · Anti-hallucination, end-to-end

—

design exists, implementation pending

All the other tests measure whether the right document is findable. This test measures whether an AI coding agent, given cupertino's top results, actually produces Swift that compiles, calls real APIs, and respects platform availability. The actual success measure for cupertino. Design written; implementation is the next 1-2 weeks of work (issue #816).

Read the design
callout_html:
One architectural pattern surfaces twice

The two weak tests (abbreviations and symbol attributes) both reveal the same gap: cupertino has rich relational metadata (synonym tables, symbol kinds, attributes, conformances) that the default search path doesn't consult. A consolidated fix likely closes both. Tracking issues are open with three candidate approaches each.

Related issues: #818 acronym routing #819 attribute filters #820 design-vocab intent #821 prose profile
